content.text: Your maps are the briefing.

The Guide owns the where: maps, safe passage, and Char route knowledge — the route faucet, and the convoy economy's native trade alongside the Rigger. It bleeds from Awareness and Rift Lore. The rank ladder reads in routes: a Sideline knows some shortcuts; an Established Guide gets asked for by route; a Name's maps are the briefing — corp logistics buys the survey data and pretends it was theirs.
In-world, mission booklets are Guide work product — the map and route cards a squad navigates by are the same artifact a Guide sells (see Intel and Corp Briefings).

content.title: Guide
content.subtitle: The route faucet

entries.label: Field verb
entries.value: scouting nodes on mission (+1 PP, max 1/mission)

content.desc: PP economy, tiers, and the One Name rule live on Professions Overview. Like the Carver, the Guide's office is the job — field scouting levels the trade.

content.text: Perks — Sideline
The Long Way Round (entry, 4 PP): opens the trade — the Work verb and the field verb are yours.
Perks — Established
Trail Sense (T2, 4 PP): never lost; known terrain travels one period faster.
Route Book (T2, 5 PP): your maps are sellable assets, and one free node Tag in surveyed territory per mission (intel states live on Intel).
Gate Whisperer (T2, 6 PP): checkpoints read your party one suspicion level lower. Obligation: gate officers expect their gifts.
Char Legs (T2, 5 PP): compressed-route legs take −1 risk tick (the Char and its 5:1 compression live on Vehicles).
Perks — Name
The Briefing Trade (T3, 7 PP): the squad's entire starting intel upgrades one state. Obligation: corp logistics buys your surveys and pretends they were theirs.
Hidden Veins (T3, 6 PP): once per arc, an unmapped route — bypass one node or gate entirely.
Guide's Name (T3, 8 PP): expeditions hire you by reputation; your maps are now corp intelligence targets.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Gate Whisperer references a checkpoint suspicion ladder and Char Legs references convoy-leg risk ticks; neither scale is printed in the rebuild docs.
What the Guide touches
The lane split: the Guide owns where; the Fixer owns who (see Fixer).
Intel states (Unknown → Tagged → Scouted → Marked) and the faucets live on Intel; the Guide's perks move the squad along that ladder before boots hit ground.
The convoy economy: Char routes, legs, and gates live on Vehicles — the Guide knows the way, the Rigger keeps the wheels under it (see Rigger).
Survey data received. Per standard acquisition terms, authorship transfers on payment. You were never here, and neither was the route.
